title: Thelypteris eriocarpa
intro: Thelypteris eriocarpa, also known as the woolly maiden fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris eriocarpa include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Thelypteris eriocarpa is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
taxonomy: Thelypteris eriocarpa is classified within the plant family Thelypteridaceae and is a species of fern. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species is further categorized as a vascular plant, specifically a type of leptosporangiate fern. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its leaf structure and sporangium arrangement. The family Thelypteridaceae is itself part of the larger class Polypodiopsida, which comprises a wide range of fern species.
